title: octoknema affinis
intro: Octoknema affinis, also known as the poison tree or poison nut, is a plant species in the family Olacaceae, native to tropical Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to the presence of alkaloids and saponins. The health benefits of Octoknema affinis include its potential to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of a decoction or infusion, and it is also used as an ingredient in some traditional remedies.
